fault_hypothesis: Lines 81-82 incorrectly remove the last element when both streams end with the same price, but the merge loop already appended both copies correctly. This post-processing pop is wrong and causes the duplicate tail price to be lost.

constraints.intent: After the pop, the merged list should still have len(left)+len(right) elements, but the pop incorrectly removes one.
Inside the then-branch that pops an element, the total count must still equal the sum of both input lengths.
At each loop iteration end, the number of merged elements must equal the sum of the two pointer positions.
Immediately after initialization, merged must be empty.
The returned merged list must contain exactly as many elements as both input lists combined.
